title: A Great and Mighty Wonder

w: The In- fant born in Beth- l'em, the Sa- vior and the Lord. 
w: And Christ shall wield His scep- ter, our Lord and God for aye.

metadata.composer: 'Es Ist Ein Ros Entsprungen (Rhythmic)' German from Köln, 1599 Michael Praetorius, 1609
metadata.lyricist: Germanus of Constantinople (634-734).  Translated by John Mason Neale, 1862

c_lines: Words: Germanus of Constantinople (634-734).  Translated by John Mason Neale, 1862.
Music: 'Es Ist Ein Ros Entsprungen (Rhythmic)' German from Köln, 1599.  Setting: Michael Praetorius, 1609.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2010 Revision.

1.raw_text: A great and mighty wonder, a full and holy cure: The virgin bears the Infant with virgin honor pure! Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

2.raw_text: The Word becomes incarnate and yet remains on high, And cheru- bim sing anthems to shepherds from the sky. Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

3.raw_text: While thus they sing your Monarch, those bright angelic bands, Rejoice, ye vales and mountains, ye oceans, clap your hands. Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

4.raw_text: Since all He comes to ransom, by all be He adored, The Infant born in Bethl'em, the Savior and the Lord. Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

5.raw_text: And idol forms shall perish, and error shall decay, And Christ shall wield His scepter, our Lord and God for aye. Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"
